title: Agency Dashboard
description: One high-level place to see every client website, who owns it, whether it is healthy, where it is hosted, and what needs attention next.
noteBody: Keep Aperix-Website public. Build this as Aperix-Admin and progressively sync it with GitHub, Netlify, Cloudflare, and your lead pipeline.

stats.label: Total Websites
stats.description: Live clients, internal demos, active builds, and maintenance sites.

stats.label: Healthy / Live
stats.description: Passed recent checks for uptime, SSL, deploy freshness, and domain routing.

stats.label: Needs Attention
stats.description: Hosting, content, or approval tasks waiting on action this week.

stats.label: Owner Split
stats.description: Lead ownership balance across the current portfolio.

board.title: Website Portfolio Board
board.description: This is the main dashboard board. Each site shows lead owner, status, stack, and last deploy freshness at a glance.

overview.title: What this page does
overview.description: Fast daily triage. You open this first and immediately know what is healthy, who owns what, and where to click next.

items.title: Morning scan
items.description: Check alerts, recent deploys, and any site marked as needing attention.

items.title: Ownership clarity
items.description: Every project shows lead owner and support owner so both of you know who is driving it.

items.title: One-click drilldown
items.description: Open the client detail page for hosting, repo, DNS, launch tasks, and linked dashboards.

activity.title: Rhino's Walk deployed
activity.detail: main — Update hero copy and add team photo

activity.title: Lumina approval pending
activity.detail: Client stakeholder review — Thomas needs to follow up

activity.title: Aperix Website deployed
activity.detail: main — Update services pricing and tier cards

activity.title: Contact env audit needed
activity.detail: Aperix production Resend env vars need review

design.title: Design idea
design.description: Aperix-like editorial glass UI, but more operational and dense. Calm layout, high-signal cards, and no clutter.
design.callout: In the real app, these cards would be hydrated from your internal client records and later enhanced with GitHub, Netlify, and Cloudflare API data.
